cityDNA.coreFantasy: A marsupial red-rock caravan city where caravanserais, covered bazaars, assay halls, terrace granaries, aid commons, sun-disc registry law, and the Dream-Burrow Hall turn red earth, trade, kinship, taxation, provision, and refuge into civic order.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Warrakai is tough, generous, and transactional at once; survival is measured in routes, shade, seed, and who gets brought inside.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Warrakai is the architecture of red-earth shelter: a marsupial beastfolk city that turns routes, trade, tax, seed, aid, ancestry, and canyon survival into civic order.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Sandstone, red rock, eucalyptus-dark timber, canvas shade, red awnings, canyon bridges, caravan yards, covered bazaars, assay counters, terraced gardens, ration arcades, sun-disc courts, and dream-burrow halls.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Marsupial beastfolk civic culture shaped by family clusters, dryland travel, market trust, route rights, seed security, public aid, and ancestral burrow memory.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Dream-burrows, sun discs, caravan beacons, covered bazaars, assay seals, terrace granaries, ration arcades, and red-earth halls symbolize shelter as both kinship and law.

canonLocks: Warrakai is a settled marsupial desert/canyon civilization, not temporary frontier settlement.
The Wonder is Marsupial Dream-Burrow Hall of Red Earth.
The Seat of Power is Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court.
The five primary districts are Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Treasury / Taxation, Agricultural Terraces, and Refugee Commons.

Exact uploaded anchors include Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, Caravanserai Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, Waystation Hall Nook, Grand Market Civic Hall, Covered Bazaar, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook, Treasury / Taxation Hall, Assay Hall, Gold-Seal Monument, Taxation Arcade Nook, Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook, Refugee Commons Civic Hall, Aid Commons, Woven Banners Monument, and Ration Arcade Nook.
provisionalCanon: Warrakai's highest civic virtue is shelter.
Trade calculation and kinship obligation should remain in tension across Warrakai scenes.
cautionFlags: Write marsupial bodies through posture, pouches, family clusters, hopping or long-stride routes, shaded resting platforms, and burrow-like public spaces, not novelty.
Keep Aid Commons morally central rather than a background charity space.

environment.environmentalLogic: Canyon, dry wadi, red earth, and shade are civic materials. The city survives by routing movement, pricing goods, storing seed, and sheltering displaced kin inside hard desert terrain.

forms: Marsupial Dream-Burrow Hall of Red Earth
Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
skyline: Dream-Burrow Hall rising from red canyon as wonder anchor
Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court as formal authority
covered bazaars, assay halls, terrace granaries, and aid arcades embedded in sandstone streets

civilization.people: Citizens are marsupial beastfolk: resilient, heat-adapted, family-clustered, route-aware, market-practical, and strongly oriented around sheltering young and kin.
civilization.governance: Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court governs movement, market standing, tax status, seed stores, aid eligibility, residency, and ancestral claims through public record.
civilization.religion: Dream-burrow ancestry, red-earth shelter rites, sun-disc judgment, seed continuity, and kin-refuge obligations make survival sacred-adjacent.

streets.appearance: Dry, red, shaded, busy, and kin-conscious: canyon walls, timber awnings, caravan posts, bazaar shade, assay counters, terraced gardens, aid lines, and dream-burrow motifs under harsh clear light.
streets.movement: Movement follows caravan gates, covered bazaars, canyon stairs, assay hall steps, terrace paths, aid lines, shade routes, and side burrow passages.
streets.lighting: Hard red daylight is controlled by canvas shade, awnings, registry glare, and warm lanterns at night.
streets.weatherEffects: Dry heat and dust turn shade, cistern routes, terrace irrigation, and aid lines into core movement logic.
streets.dayCycle: Caravan arrivals, market bargaining, assay work, terrace labor, and aid distribution dominate the day.
streets.nightCycle: Cooler caravan traffic, lantern-lit bazaars, ration checks, and dream-burrow gatherings dominate night.

housing.wealthy: Registry and caravan-house residences built into canyon terraces with shaded courtyards, private water, red-stone arches, and eucalyptus beams.
housing.middle: Bazaar, assay, and terrace-family homes with shade cloth, storage alcoves, and shared water routes.
housing.poor: Aid-adjacent shelter courts, red-rock rooms, patched awnings, ration lines, and kin clusters under public shade.

districts.name: Transit / Caravan / Rail
districts.civicPurpose: Arrival, route law, caravan coordination, and public signal response.
districts.visualIdentity: Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, Caravanserai Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, Waystation Hall Nook.

districts.name: Grand Market
districts.civicPurpose: Trade, shade economy, auction pressure, and public bargaining.
districts.visualIdentity: Grand Market Civic Hall, Covered Bazaar, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook.

districts.name: Treasury / Taxation
districts.civicPurpose: Valuation, tribute, assay credibility, tax pressure, and fraud detection.
districts.visualIdentity: Treasury / Taxation Hall, Assay Hall, Gold-Seal Monument, Taxation Arcade Nook.

districts.name: Agricultural Terraces
districts.civicPurpose: Dryland food security, seed continuity, and terrace labor.
districts.visualIdentity: Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook.

districts.name: Refugee Commons
districts.civicPurpose: Refugee care, ration pressure, public obligation, and moral testing of kinship law.
districts.visualIdentity: Refugee Commons Civic Hall, Aid Commons, Woven Banners Monument, Ration Arcade Nook.

wonder.name: Marsupial Dream-Burrow Hall of Red Earth
wonder.symbolicRole: Ancestral burrow, red earth, kin shelter, and dream-guided civic memory made public.
wonder.visualRole: Wonder-scale red-earth hall rising from canyon stone as shelter turned into government.
wonder.civicRole: Public rites of refuge, ancestry, displacement, kinship, and the moral meaning of home.
storyHooks: A dream-burrow record names someone as kin before the registry does.

beacon.name: Caravanserai Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Route warning and caravan coordination as the city’s public signal heart.
beacon.visualRole: Beacon rising from caravan yards, waystation halls, woven banners, and canyon approaches.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates caravans, bazaar stalls, assay clerks, terrace stores, and aid commons immediately.
storyHooks: A Beacon change makes every route contract suddenly personal.

seatOfPower.name: Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Public record, sun judgment, trade status, and refuge eligibility made government.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Sun-disc registry court of pale stone, red-earth approaches, sealed records, and public stairs.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Disputes over caravan rights, taxation, aid, seed stores, residency, and ancestral claims.
storyHooks: A registry category decides whether a stranger becomes cargo, client, or kin.

majorLandmarks.name: Covered Bazaar
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Covered bazaar with red awnings, auction court, shaded aisles, and market crowds.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Trade, bargaining, auction pressure, and public rumor.
storyHooks: An auction phrase reveals who truly owns the shade.

majorLandmarks.name: Assay Hall
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Trust measured, taxed, and sometimes manipulated.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Assay hall with gold-seal monument, taxation arcade, ledgers, counters, and hard civic stone.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Valuation, taxation, trade credibility, and fraud discovery.
storyHooks: An assay mark proves the aid ledger is wrong.

majorLandmarks.name: Terrace Granary
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Terrace granary with hanging gardens, seed hall, irrigation, sacks, and canyon steps.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Seed continuity, food storage, and dryland agriculture.
storyHooks: A seed hall entry proves the city expected exile before it admitted refugees.

majorLandmarks.name: Aid Commons
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Shelter as public obligation rather than private kindness.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Aid commons with ration arcade, banners, shade, water jars, and waiting kin groups.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Refugee care, ration pressure, public obligation, and moral accounting.
storyHooks: A ration token marks who the city is willing to call family.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, Caravanserai Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, and Waystation Hall Nook.
S2 must show Grand Market Civic Hall, Covered Bazaar, Awning Monument, and Auction Court Nook.
S3 must show Treasury / Taxation Hall, Assay Hall, Gold-Seal Monument, and Taxation Arcade Nook.
S4 must show Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary, Hanging Gardens Monument, and Seed Hall Nook.
S5 must show Refugee Commons Civic Hall, Aid Commons, Woven Banners Monument, and Ration Arcade Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Keep Marsupial Dream-Burrow Hall of Red Earth, Caravanserai Beacon, and Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court legible together.
Use sandstone, canyon shade, eucalyptus timber, red awnings, caravan yards, covered bazaars, assay counters, terrace granaries, ration arcades, and dream-burrow motifs.
requiredVisualElements: Marsupial Dream-Burrow Hall of Red Earth
Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
elementsToAvoid: assay/taxation softened into generic market activity
